EXHIBITS A-G FOR DECLARATION OF GEOFFREY SWENSON IN OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANTS MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS (TRANSACTION ID # 100132226) FILED BY PLAINTIFF SWENSON, GEOFFREY RICHARD AN INDIVIDUAL

DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, JUN-02-2021 HEARING HELD RE: DEFENDANT DANIEL SWENSON'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AND DENIED AS FOLLOWS: AS TO 3, 4, 8, 12 THE MOTION IS GRANTED: DANIEL ALLEGES THAT GEOFFREY COMINGLED PERSONAL ASSETS AND THAT A FORENSIC ACCOUNTANT FOUND THAT "GEOFFREY HAD IMPROPERLY MADE LOANS TO RELATED PARTIES, INCLUDING HIMSELF, HIS OTHER BUSINESSES.GEOFFREY CAUSED BETTY'S BOYS TO BEAR THE BURDEN OF SERVICING DEBT FOR WHICH GEOFFREY WAS THE OBLIGOR, INCLUDING A HOME EQUITY LINE WHICH GEOFFREY USED TO FUND HIS INITIAL CASH CONTRIBUTION TO THE COMPANY" (XC, PARA. 18). AS TO 3, 8, 12, THE RFPS ARE LIMITED TO 2008 FORWARD. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO 1, 14, 15, 16, AS GEOFFREY PROVIDED CODE-COMPLAINT RESPONSES. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO 5, 6, AND 9-NO ALLEGATIONS IN THE XC AGAINST GRS RESTAURANT GROUP, INC. HAVE BEEN IDENTIFIED AND NO EVIDENCE OF GRS RESTAURANT GROUP, INC.'S INVOLVEMENT HAS BEEN PROVIDED IN SUPPORT OF THE MOTION. GEOFFREY'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF TO SERVE FURTHER RESPONSES WITHIN TWO WEEKS OF THE NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THE COURT'S ORDER. DEFENSE COUNSEL IS TO PREPARE AND EMAIL A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT PETER.CATALANOTTI@WILSONELSER.COM BY NOON TODAY (CC'ING THE OPPOSING SIDE). JUDGE PRO TEM: PETER CATALANOTTI; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/EPS)

Motion To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-27-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS GEOFFREY SWENSON and BETTY'S BOYS, INC.'S Motion To File First Amended Complaint.

No appearances are made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Geoffrey Swenson's motion to file first amended complaint is granted. The motion is supported by the strong policy of liberality in permitting amendments to pleadings. Defendant has not shown that plaintiff unduly delayed in seeking leave to amend, nor that he will be prejudiced by the proposed amendment.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Aug-12-2021 Continued To Jan-18-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Jun-02-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. CHARLES J. KATZ, Esq. of Law Offices of Charles J. Katz (Tel: 650-692-4100) for Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Geoffrey Swenson;
Atty. JUSTIN L. SOWA, Esq. of Gluck Daniel LLP (Tel: 415-510-2114) for Defendant/Cross-Complainant Daniel Swenson.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT DANIEL SWENSON's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for parties authorize the clerk to sign the stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The Motion is Granted and Denied as follows: As to 3, 4, 8, 12 the Motion is Granted: Daniel alleges that Geoffrey comingled personal assets and that a forensic accountant found that "Geoffrey had improperly made loans to related parties, including himself, his other businesses.Geoffrey caused Betty's Boys to bear the burden of servicing debt for which Geoffrey was the obligor, including a home equity line which Geoffrey used to fund his initial cash contribution to the company" (XC, Para. 18). As to 3, 8, 12, the RFPs are limited to 2008 forward. The Motion is Denied as to 1, 14, 15, 16, as Geoffrey provided Code-Complaint responses. The Motion is Denied as to 5, 6, and 9-No allegations in the XC against GRS Restaurant Group, Inc. have been identified and no evidence of GRS Restaurant Group, Inc.'s involvement has been provided in support of the Motion. Geoffrey's request for sanctions is denied. Plaintiff to serve further responses within two weeks of the notice of entry of the Court's order.

Defense counsel is to prepare and email a proposed order to the Judge Pro Tem at Peter.Catalanotti@wilsonelser.com by noon today (cc'ing the opposing side).

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/EPS)
